pairs.question: Who was Anilus Joseph and what Olympic event did he compete in?
pairs.answer: Anilus Joseph was a Haitian track and field athlete born on January 21, 1945. He competed in the 10,000 metres event at the 1972 Olympic Games in Munich.

pairs.question: How did Anilus Joseph perform during his first Olympic 10,000 metres race?
pairs.answer: Joseph started quickly, covering the first lap in 59.6 seconds. However, he was passed by all other competitors by the eighth lap and passed again by the 12th lap. After being incorrectly told he still had a mile to go during the final sprint, he retired from the race.

pairs.question: What was the significance of the Haitian long-distance track and field team in the 1970s and 1980s?
pairs.answer: The Haitian team, delegated by the Baby Doc Duvalier regime, became notorious for setting some of the all-time worst times in Olympic history. Many of these records remain unbroken to this day.

pairs.question: Name two other Haitian athletes who competed in the Olympics during the 1970s and 1980s alongside Anilus Joseph.
pairs.answer: Two notable performers were Wilnor Joseph, who ran the 800 metres in 2:15.26 in 1976, and Dieudonné LaMothe, who finished last in both the 5000 metres in 1976 and the marathon in 1984.

pairs.question: How did Charles Olemus distinguish himself during the 1976 Olympics?
pairs.answer: Charles Olemus disrupted the track and field schedule for 15 minutes when he blocked the entire field by covering the last six laps of his 10,000 metres race.

pairs.question: Why is the Haitian Olympic team of the 1970s and 1980s often remembered for poor performance?
pairs.answer: The team is infamous for consistently producing some of the slowest times in Olympic history, many of which have not been surpassed. Their performances are frequently cited as examples of historically poor athletic outcomes.
